color.$description: background/surface/foreground/analogous-0..2/border are pinned literal hex (independently authored / design.google-matched neutrals and decorative hues). accent/accent-active/focus-ring/link/link-hover are the brand hue — literal #f26b22 orange per explicit user direction (2026-07-15), with accent-active a lightness-raised HSL variant of the same hue for hover emphasis. success/warning/danger are core-ramp aliases: warningRamp was hue-rotated to a clear yellow (~50°) so it reads distinctly from the orange accent instead of the two colliding (both previously sat in the amber/gold 35-51° range) — genuine green/yellow/red, not the decorative analogous-0..2 family. All verified >= WCAG AA contrast against near-black text at these steps.

surface-plain.$description: Deliberately identical to `surface` (not `background`) in dark theme — per docs/design-research/elevation-surface-case-study.md, dark mode keeps the validated pattern where a card's resting state is already lifted off the page background, since shadows barely register on a dark base and the lightening-with-elevation tint needs headroom above something other than the plain background. Light theme's surface-plain instead matches `background`, not `surface` — the two themes deliberately diverge here, see tokens/semantic/light.json's surface-plain description.

surface-tint.$description: The lighten-with-elevation tint mixed into --cr-color-surface-1..5 (see generated/css/light.css and dark.css) — matches foreground exactly, deliberately: dark-theme elevation lightens toward white, same direction as Material 2/YouTube's white-alpha-on-dark technique.
